summary: Iter74 frontend-only verification of tab-strip mobile scroll + inline PDF rendering + Finance invoice modal/row responsive fixes. Login OK. 15 tab-strip snapshots across (Outreach/Team/Clients/Projects/WebsiteConfig) x (375/800/1280) — all clean. Playbook PDF renders 2 canvases inline @375+1280 with Download/New-tab header buttons non-overlapping. Finance invoice preview modal @375 opens, renders 1 canvas inline, and header wraps title on top + Download/Send/WhatsApp/close on their own row (zero overlap, tested across all 5 finance projects). Invoice list rows @375 are flex-wrap:wrap, height 130-187px, scrollW==clientW==299 (fits 375 viewport), no amount-vs-date overlap. Documents PDF preview @375 (Performance_Certificate(5).pdf) renders 2 canvases inline. Zero console/page errors, zero page-level horizontal overflow at 375/800.
critical_code_review_comments: index.css tab-strip selector — uses attribute-substring matching on Tailwind class names ([class*='flex'][class*='rounded-full'][class*='p-1'][class*='ring-slate-200']). This works today but is brittle: any pill tab that drops the ring-slate-200 (or uses ring-slate-100/300) will silently opt out of the horizontal scroll behaviour. Recommend switching to an explicit selector like `.pill-tab-strip` or `[data-tab-strip]` and adding that marker to the 5+ tab-bar components, so this contract is intentional and greppable.

PdfCanvasViewer.jsx renders every page at up-to 900px scaled and DPR-clamped to 2. Fine for the small PDFs in this app but a 20+ page PDF (or a 6MB scan) will noticeably delay first paint because it's a serial for-loop. Consider (a) rendering only the first page eagerly + lazy-render the rest on scroll via IntersectionObserver, or (b) rendering all pages in parallel with Promise.all — either would improve perceived performance for larger PDFs.

PdfCanvasViewer scale calc uses `Math.max((width - 8) / vp1.width, 0.3)` — the 0.3 floor prevents zero-width canvases when holder.clientWidth briefly reports 0 during mount, good. But there's no upper cap either, so on ultra-wide desktop (>1200px inside the modal) the canvas can exceed the 900px `width` cap — actually width is already Math.min(clientWidth, 900) so this is fine. Comment-worthy only.

Finance.jsx invoice-preview modal is now well-behaved on mobile (title row + button row wrap cleanly), but the file is 1700+ lines and the local PdfPreviewModal duplicates logic already living in the shared /components/PdfPreviewModal.jsx. Consider consolidating to a single shared modal so future PDF UX changes only need to touch one place.

PdfCanvasViewer worker path uses `${process.env.PUBLIC_URL || ''}/pdf.worker.min.mjs`. PUBLIC_URL is empty in this CRA build → resolves to `/pdf.worker.min.mjs`. Works because the file is served from public/. If the app is ever deployed under a subpath, PUBLIC_URL must be set at build time or the worker will 404 and pdf.js will silently fall back to fake-worker mode (much slower). Worth documenting in README.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — 15/15 tab-strip snapshots, 2/2 playbook PDF inline (mobile+desktop), 4/4 finance invoice-modal (4 cards had invoices), 8+/8+ invoice row wraps, 1/1 Documents PDF, 0 receipts existed to test

seed_data_creation: No data created or modified. Only read/preview actions taken (opened + closed modals). All 5 existing finance projects, 8 existing invoices, and 10 existing documents left untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Iter74 responsive fixes verified. Mobile 375px tab-strip contract: any .portal-scope div with classes {flex, rounded-full, p-1, ring-slate-200} becomes flex-wrap:nowrap overflow-x:auto with edge-fade mask under <1024px. Verified strips: Outreach (10 pills, scrollW=1166), Team (5 pills, scrollW=568 — Permission Matrix last tab reachable + clickable), Clients (3 pills, scrollW=394), Projects (3 pills, scrollW=343 — no scroll needed), WebsiteConfig (5 pills, scrollW=618). Tablet 800px: same nowrap+scroll behavior, no page overflow. Desktop 1280px: overflow-x:visible, flex-wrap:wrap resumes — no regression. PdfCanvasViewer selector: [data-testid='pdf-canvas-viewer'] contains N <canvas> children once ready — typical render time 1-2s for 2-page PDFs in this env, use 12-15s max wait. PdfPreviewModal: [data-testid='pdf-preview-modal']; Finance invoice modal: [data-testid='invoice-preview-modal']. Finance invoice rows on mobile: display:flex, flexWrap:wrap, h≈130-187px depending on badges; all under 300px inner width comfortably. No receipts exist in any of the 5 finance projects — main agent may need to create one via the approve-payment flow if that surface needs testing. Playbook PDFs: only pb-open-client-onboarding-playbook renders (pb-open-projexino-hub not found on this env — spec called out expected id may have been renamed or is dynamic). Documents PDF: Performance_Certificate(5).pdf renders 2 canvases inline.
rca_of_the_issue: No issues found. The 4 fixes described in the review request are all working as intended: (1) global CSS pill-strip rule turns every ragged wrap into a single-row horizontal-scroll strip with edge fade under 1024px; (2) PdfCanvasViewer.jsx cleanly renders every PDF page as a full-width <canvas> inline, works on both mobile playbook cover and desktop; (3) Finance local PdfPreviewModal header now uses a title row + button row that wrap independently (Download/Send/WhatsApp/close never overlap the title on 375px); (4) invoice list rows are flex-wrap:wrap at mobile widths so text {invoice-no + dates + status + amount} flow cleanly without amount overlapping dates. Zero console errors, zero page-level horizontal overflow, zero React error overlays anywhere during the traversal.
